Results.Query: Write an apology letter to a friend whose birthday I missed
Results.ActualAnswer:
Here's a heartfelt apology letter you can personalize for your friend:

---

**Subject: I'm So Sorry I Missed Your Birthday**

Dear [Friend's Name],

I am writing this with a heavy heart because I truly cannot believe I missed your birthday. There is absolutely no excuse for it, and I want you to know how genuinely sorry I am.

Your birthday is a day that deserves to be celebrated, and the fact that I wasn't there — even just to send a message — is something I deeply regret. You mean so much to me, and my absence on such a special day was never a reflection of how much I value you and our friendship.

I know a belated "sorry" doesn't make up for the missed moment, but I want you to know that I am truly ashamed of my oversight. You have always been there for me through so much, and you deserved better from me on your day.

I would love to make it up to you — whether that's treating you to dinner, celebrating together, or simply spending some quality time catching up. You deserve to be celebrated, and I intend to do just that, even if it's a little late.

Please know that this will not happen again. I value our friendship far too much to let life's distractions get in the way of the people who matter most to me — and you are absolutely one of those people.

Once again, I am truly sorry. I hope you had a wonderful birthday despite my absence, and I hope you can find it in your heart to forgive me.

With love and sincere apologies,
[Your Name]

---

> 💡 **Tips to personalize this letter:**
> - **Add a specific memory** you share with your friend to make it more heartfelt.
> - **Mention their name** throughout to keep it personal.
> - **Include a concrete plan** to make it up to them (e.g., a specific outing or gift).
> - **Adjust the tone** — keep it warm and casual if you have a lighthearted friendship, or more sincere if the situation calls for it.

Results.Query: What year did World War II end?
Results.ActualAnswer:
World War II ended in **1945**. More specifically:

- **In Europe**, the war ended in **May 1945**, following the surrender of Nazi Germany after Adolf Hitler's suicide on April 30. [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/End_of_World_War_II_in_Europe)
- **In Asia**, the war officially ended on **September 2, 1945**, with Japan's formal surrender aboard the USS Missouri. [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/End_of_World_War_II_in_Asia)

Results.Query: Who invented the telephone?
Results.ActualAnswer:
The invention of the telephone is attributed to multiple figures, but the most recognized is **Alexander Graham Bell**:

- **Alexander Graham Bell**, a Scottish-born inventor, scientist, and engineer, was **the first to be granted a United States patent** for a device that produced clearly intelligible replication of the human voice at a second device, **in 1876** [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Telephone). He also co-founded the American Telephone and Telegraph Company (AT&T) [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alexander_Graham_Bell).

- However, **Antonio Meucci**, an Italian inventor, is also widely recognized as a key pioneer of voice communication technology and is credited by some as the true inventor of the telephone [3](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Antonio_Meucci). In fact, the U.S. House of Representatives passed a resolution in 2002 honoring Meucci's contributions.

In summary, **Alexander Graham Bell** holds the first practical telephone **patent**, but **Antonio Meucci** is also historically recognized as a foundational inventor of the device.

Results.Query: What is the speed of light?
Results.ActualAnswer:
The speed of light in a vacuum, commonly denoted **c**, is exactly **299,792,458 metres per second (m/s)** — roughly **1 billion kilometres per hour** or **700 million miles per hour** [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speed_of_light).

This value is fixed by definition: a metre is defined as the distance light travels in a vacuum in 1/299,792,458 of a second [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speed_of_light).

Here are a few key facts about *c*:

- **Universal speed limit:** It is the upper limit for the speed at which information, matter, or energy can travel through space [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speed_of_light).
- **Applies to all electromagnetic radiation:** Visible light, radio waves, X-rays, and other forms of electromagnetic radiation all travel at *c* in a vacuum, as do massless particles and gravitational waves [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speed_of_light).
- **Observer-independent:** The speed of light is the same for all observers, regardless of their relative velocity or the motion of the light source [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speed_of_light).
- **Slower in materials:** Light travels slower than *c* when passing through transparent materials like glass (~200,000 km/s) or air (~90 km/s slower than *c*) [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speed_of_light).
- **Famous equation:** Einstein's mass–energy equivalence, **E = mc²**, features *c* as a fundamental constant linking space, time, mass, and energy [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speed_of_light).

Results.Query: Tell me about Jordan
Results.ActualAnswer:
The search returned results for both the **country of Jordan** and **Michael Jordan** (the basketball player). Here's a detailed overview of the **country**:

---

## 🇯🇴 Jordan (Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan)

### Geography
Jordan, officially the **Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan**, is a country in the **Southern Levant region of West Asia**. It is bordered by Syria to the north, Iraq to the east, Saudi Arabia to the south, and both Israel and Palestine (West Bank) to the west. [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jordan) The Jordan River, flowing into the Dead Sea, runs along its western border, and the country has a small coastline along the **Red Sea** in the southwest, at the Gulf of Aqaba. [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jordan) It covers an area of **89,342 km²** and is considered a **semi-arid** country. [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jordan)

### Capital & Population
**Amman** is the country's capital and largest city, and the most populous city in the Levant. [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jordan) Jordan has a population of approximately **11.5 million**, making it the eleventh-most populous Arab country. [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jordan)

### History
- Humans have inhabited the region since the **Paleolithic period**. During the Iron Age, three kingdoms emerged: **Ammon, Moab, and Edom**. [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jordan)
- In the 3rd century BC, the **Arab Nabataeans** established their kingdom centered in **Petra**. [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jordan)
- The region later passed through Byzantine, Islamic Caliphate (Rashidun, Umayyad, Abbasid), and Ottoman rule. [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jordan)
- Following the **1916 Great Arab Revolt** in WWI, the **Emirate of Transjordan** was established in 1921 as a British protectorate. [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jordan)
- Jordan gained full **independence in 1946** and became the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan. [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jordan)
- Jordan signed a **peace treaty with Israel in 1994** and renounced its claim to the West Bank in **1988**. [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jordan)

### Government
Jordan is a **semi-constitutional monarchy**, where the king holds wide executive and legislative powers. [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jordan) It is a founding member of the **Arab League** and the **Organisation of Islamic Cooperation**. [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jordan)

### Society & Religion
About **95% of the population is Sunni Muslim**, with the remainder being mostly Arab Christians. [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jordan) Jordan has historically been a major refuge for displaced populations, hosting an estimated **2.1 million Palestinian refugees** and **1.4 million Syrian refugees** (as of 2015). [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jordan)

### Economy
Jordan has a **lower middle income economy** and ranks **100th on the Human Development Index**. [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jordan) It is a major **tourist destination** and attracts **medical tourism** due to its well-developed health sector. However, a **lack of natural resources**, large refugee inflows, and regional instability have hampered economic growth. [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jordan)

---

> **Note:** If you were asking about **Michael Jordan** (the basketball legend) or something else entirely, please let me know and I'll look that up for you!

Results.Query: Tell me about Apple, the famous technology company
Results.ActualAnswer:
## Apple Inc.

**Overview**
Apple Inc. is an American multinational technology company headquartered in Cupertino, California, in Silicon Valley, known for consumer electronics, software, and online services. It is one of the "Big Tech" companies and has consistently been ranked as one of the world's most valuable brands since the late 2000s. [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_Inc.)

---

### 🏛️ Founding & Early History
Apple was **founded in 1976** as Apple Computer Company by **Steve Jobs, Steve Wozniak, and Ronald Wayne**. It was incorporated the following year and renamed to its current name, Apple Inc., in **2007** as the company expanded its focus from computers to consumer electronics. [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_Inc.)

The company was originally founded to market **Wozniak's Apple I** personal computer. Its successor, the **Apple II**, became one of the first successful mass-produced microcomputers. Apple then introduced the **Lisa in 1983** and the iconic **Macintosh in 1984** — among the first computers to use a **graphical user interface and a mouse**. [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_Inc.)

---

### 📉 Turbulent Years & Revival
By 1985, internal conflicts led to Jobs leaving to form **NeXT**, while Wozniak withdrew to other ventures. Throughout the 1990s, Apple lost significant market share in the PC industry and **neared bankruptcy by 1997**. The turnaround came when Apple **acquired NeXT**, bringing Jobs back. Under his renewed leadership, Apple returned to profitability by introducing: [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_Inc.)
- The **iMac**, **iPod**, **iPhone**, and **iPad**
- The **iTunes Store**
- The **"Think Different"** advertising campaign
- The **Apple Store** retail chain

---

### 📱 Products & Services
Apple's current product lineup includes: [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_Inc.)
- **Hardware:** iPhone, iPad, Mac, Apple Watch, AirPods
- **Operating Systems:** iOS, iPadOS, macOS
- **Services & Software:** Apple Pay, iCloud, Apple Music, Apple TV

---

### 💰 Scale & Market Position
Apple is a financial powerhouse: [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_Inc.)
- **Since 2011**, it has been, for the most part, the **world's largest company by market capitalization**
- As of 2024, it is the **largest manufacturing company by revenue**, the **largest vendor of tablet computers**, and the **largest vendor of mobile phones**
- It became the **first publicly traded US company valued at over $1 trillion** in 2018
- As of **October 2025**, Apple is valued at just over **$4 trillion**

---

### 👔 Leadership
Jobs resigned as CEO in 2011 for health reasons and **passed away the same year**. He was succeeded by **Tim Cook**, who is set to be succeeded by **John Ternus in 2026**. [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_Inc.)

---

### ⚠️ Criticism
Despite its success, Apple has faced criticism over **contractors' labor conditions**, **environmental practices**, **anti-competitive tactics**, and its **acquisitions of smaller businesses**. Nevertheless, the company maintains a large following and enjoys a **high level of customer loyalty**. [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_Inc.)

Results.Query: Did Einstein win a Nobel Prize for the theory of relativity?
Results.ActualAnswer:
No. Einstein did **not** win the Nobel Prize for the theory of relativity. He received the **1921 Nobel Prize in Physics** specifically for **"his services to theoretical physics, and especially for his discovery of the law of the photoelectric effect"** [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Albert_Einstein).

While Einstein is best known for developing the theory of relativity [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Albert_Einstein), the Nobel Committee did not cite it as the basis for the award. The prize was instead granted for his work on the **photoelectric effect**, a foundational contribution to quantum theory.

Results.Query: Was the Great Wall of China visible from space?
Results.ActualAnswer:
**No** — the claim that the Great Wall of China is clearly visible from space, and especially the popular version that it is visible from the Moon, is a myth.

Here is what the evidence shows:

- **From the Moon:** This is false. The Great Wall enjoys "the reputation of being the only work of human hands on the globe visible from the moon," but this is a misconception. [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_structures_visible_from_space)

- **From low Earth orbit:** It is *conditionally* possible. According to astronauts **Eugene Cernan and Ed Lu**, the Great Wall is visible from the lower part of low Earth orbit, but **only under very favorable conditions**. [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_structures_visible_from_space) One account states that from an orbit of 100 to 200 miles (160–320 km) high, it is "indeed, visible to the naked eye," but notes it is "less visible than a lot of" other structures. [3](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Great_Wall_of_China)

- **Not uniquely visible:** The Great Wall is not the *only* human-made object visible from space — this too is listed as a common misconception. [4](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_common_misconceptions_about_science,_technology,_and_mathematics)

In summary: the Wall *may* be glimpsed from low Earth orbit under ideal conditions, but it is **not** visible from the Moon, and the broader popular claim is largely a myth.

Results.Query: Did Napoleon win the Battle of Waterloo?
Results.ActualAnswer:
No, Napoleon did **not** win the Battle of Waterloo — he was decisively defeated.

The Battle of Waterloo was fought on **18 June 1815**, and **"the French Imperial Army under the command of Napoleon I was defeated by two armies of the Seventh Coalition"** [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Waterloo): a British-led force under the **Duke of Wellington**, and a Prussian army under **Field Marshal Blücher.

Key outcomes:
- Wellington's forces repulsed Napoleon's final assault by the Imperial Guard, and the French army was **routed** [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Waterloo).
- **Napoleon abdicated just four days later**, and coalition forces entered Paris on 7 July 1815 [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Waterloo).
- The defeat ended Napoleon's "Hundred Days" return from exile and led to his **second and definitive abdication** as Emperor of the French, marking the end of the First French Empire [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Waterloo).

The battle was so catastrophic for Napoleon that the phrase **"meeting one's Waterloo"** became a popular expression for suffering a decisive, ruinous defeat [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Waterloo).

Results.Query: Did the Titanic sink on its first voyage?
Results.ActualAnswer: Yes. The RMS Titanic sank during its **maiden (first) voyage**. It departed from Southampton, England, bound for New York City, and was just four days into that journey when it struck an iceberg and sank in the North Atlantic Ocean in the early morning of **15 April 1912**, resulting in the deaths of over 1,500 people [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sinking_of_the_Titanic) [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crew_of_the_Titanic). The ship never completed — or made — any other voyage.

Results.Query: Did humans and dinosaurs ever co-exist?
Results.ActualAnswer:
No. Non-avian dinosaurs and modern humans did **not** coexist. The timeline gap between them is enormous:

- **Non-avian dinosaurs went extinct ~66 million years ago**, during the Cretaceous–Paleogene (K–Pg) extinction event, most likely caused by the impact of a massive asteroid 10–15 km wide that created the Chicxulub crater. [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cretaceous%E2%80%93Paleogene_extinction_event)
- **Modern humans (*Homo sapiens*)** evolved far more recently — only within the last few hundred thousand years. [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Timeline_of_human_evolution)

This means there is a gap of tens of millions of years separating the last non-avian dinosaurs from the first humans.

**Important nuance:** Dinosaurs are not entirely gone. The K–Pg extinction spared a group of avian dinosaurs — birds are, in fact, the surviving lineage of dinosaurs. In that sense, humans *do* coexist with dinosaurs today — just the feathered, living kind. [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cretaceous%E2%80%93Paleogene_extinction_event)

Results.Query: Who was the French president when the Eiffel Tower was built?
Results.ActualAnswer:
The Eiffel Tower was designed and built by Gustave Eiffel's company **from 1887 to 1889** as the centrepiece of the 1889 World's Fair [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eiffel_Tower).

During that entire construction period, **Marie François Sadi Carnot** was the President of France, serving from 1887 to 1894 [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sadi_Carnot_(statesman)). He was a statesman of the French Third Republic [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sadi_Carnot_(statesman)).

Results.Query: What is the nationality of the artist who painted the ceiling of the Sistine Chapel?
Results.ActualAnswer: The ceiling of the Sistine Chapel was painted in fresco by **Michelangelo** between 1508 and 1512 [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sistine_Chapel_ceiling). Michelangelo (1475–1564) was **Italian** — an Italian sculptor, painter, and architect of the High Renaissance [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_people_from_Italy).

Results.Query: What country was the inventor of the World Wide Web born in?
Results.ActualAnswer: Tim Berners-Lee, the inventor of the World Wide Web, was born in **England** (United Kingdom). He is described as an "English computer scientist," born on 8 June 1955 [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tim_Berners-Lee).

Results.Query: Who is the president of the country that won the 2024 T20 World Cup?
Results.ActualAnswer:
**India** won the 2024 ICC Men's T20 World Cup, defeating South Africa by 7 runs in the final held on 29 June 2024 at Kensington Oval, Barbados [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2024_Men's_T20_World_Cup_final).

The President of India is **Droupadi Murmu**, the 15th president, who has been in office since 25 July 2022 [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/President_of_India).
